posts.hook: MYTH: Eye exercises don't work. Here's what the AAO actually says.
posts.caption:
MYTH: Eye exercises are pseudoscience.

REALITY: The AAO recommends convergence exercises for convergence insufficiency. The COVD publishes guidelines for vision therapy.

Eye training won't fix your prescription — but it can reduce strain, improve convergence, and strengthen your focus muscles.

#MythBusted #EyeHealth #Axeo #ScienceTok #EyeExercises
posts.visual_notes: Text on screen 'MYTH' in red, crossed out. Cut to AAO/COVD logos with cited guidelines. Fast-paced with dramatic sound effect on myth-bust reveal.

posts.hook: 3 eye myths your optometrist wishes you'd stop believing.
posts.caption:
3 eye myths your optometrist wishes you'd stop believing:

1. Reading in the dark damages your eyes (it doesn't — just causes temporary strain)
2. Eating carrots fixes your vision (Vitamin A helps, but won't reverse refractive errors)
3. Eye exercises are useless (AAO recommends them for convergence issues)

Stop guessing. Start testing.

#EyeMyths #Axeo #AxeoVision #EyeHealth #OptometristTips #VisionCare
posts.visual_notes: Numbered list format with each myth appearing in red then getting crossed out. Replacement fact in green. Clean typography, brand colors.

posts.hook: Blue light glasses are a $30 billion scam. Here's what actually helps your eyes.
posts.caption:
Blue light glasses are a $30B industry built on fear.

The AAO says there's no evidence they prevent eye damage.

What actually helps:
- Blinking exercises
- Focus shifting
- The 20-20-20 rule

posts.hook: I tracked my eye strain for 30 days and tried structured exercises. Here's what changed.
posts.caption:
I've been dealing with digital eye strain for years — dry eyes, headaches after work, blurry distance vision by evening.

I started doing structured eye exercises (focus shifting, convergence drills, palming) for 5 minutes a day using a free browser tool.

After 30 days:
- Headaches reduced from 4x/week to 1x
- Distance blur after screen sessions mostly gone
- Actually remembering to take breaks now

Not claiming it fixes prescriptions. But for strain and fatigue, the clinical literature backs this up (AAO convergence exercise guidelines, COVD vision therapy research).

Anyone else tried structured eye exercises? Curious about your experience.

The tool I used: axeo.vision/exercise (free, browser-based)
posts.visual_notes: Text post to r/eyehealth. No images needed. Written in authentic first-person Reddit tone.
